children.finalMessage: {"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Architecture is serviceable but has several maintainability risks around process-local execution state, request-path side effects, broad module responsibilities, and hard-wired strategy extension points.","findings":[{"file":"src/server/execution/riskEngine.ts","line_start":31,"line_end":40,"severity":"high","title":"Global kill switch is process-local state","description":"GLOBAL_KILL is stored in a module variable and read by decideExecution. In a Cloudflare Worker deployment, isolates, restarts, scheduled handlers, and concurrent requests may not share this value, so the execution safety boundary is not durable or globally consistent.","recommendation":"Persist global execution state in D1/Durable Object/KV and have decideExecution read that durable source. Keep the router as a thin command handler that writes the state and audit log together."},{"file":"src/server/execution/dispatch.ts","line_start":18,"line_end":151,"severity":"high","title":"Execution dispatcher mixes durable workflow with in-memory coordination","description":"createExecutionJobsForIntent performs risk checks, credential decryption, exchange client construction, order submission, event logging, and NAV snapshots in one request-path function while using an in-memory Map for per-connection serialization. The Map does not coordinate across Worker isolates and the function is difficult to test without real database and exchange side effects.","recommendation":"Split dispatch into a durable job creator and an execution worker/adapter layer. Store queue/lock state durably, inject repositories and exchange clients, and test the risk, idempotency, and order-submission transitions independently."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":17,"line_end":278,"severity":"medium","title":"Database module has low cohesion and global dependencies","description":"db.ts owns module-level env/db state plus encryption, password hashing, audit logging, user registration, live-account operations, and demo-account operations. This makes unrelated domains depend on one large module and forces tests to mutate global setDbEnv state.","recommendation":"Move auth, crypto, audit, live-account, and demo-account operations into separate repositories/services. Pass db/env through context or explicit service constructors instead of reading module globals."},{"file":"icr_strategy/icr/signals.py","line_start":147,"line_end":299,"severity":"medium","title":"Signal construction is tightly coupled to every confluence module","description":"build_signal directly imports and invokes ICT, divergence, MTF, Coinlegs, coil gating, base scoring, target calculation, and reason formatting. Adding or testing a confluence requires editing the core signal builder and exercising the full DataFrame-oriented strategy path.","recommendation":"Introduce a ConfluenceProvider protocol and assemble enabled providers from StrategyConfig. Keep base signal detection separate from provider scoring/reason aggregation so optional modules can be tested and extended independently."},{"file":"icr_strategy/icr/main.py","line_start":27,"line_end":252,"severity":"medium","title":"CLI entry point owns too much orchestration logic","description":"main.py parses flags, builds configs, scrapes Coinlegs, downloads Binance data, loads market data, runs backtests, writes matrices, writes reports, and runs audits. This couples IO, network acquisition, research orchestration, and reporting into one module, making workflows harder to reuse or test without invoking the CLI surface.","recommendation":"Move standard backtest, Binance HTF, Coinlegs ingestion, and audit/report writing into dedicated application services. Keep parse_args/main limited to translating CLI input into service calls."}],"next_steps":["Persist execution control state and queue coordination before relying on live multi-user execution.","Refactor dispatch behind injectable repositories and exchange adapters, then add unit tests for risk/idempotency/status transitions.","Split db.ts and icr_strategy/icr/main.py along domain boundaries.","Extract strategy confluence providers from build_signal to reduce coupling and improve testability."]}

{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Security review found critical issues around deployable secrets, unauthenticated live-trading triggers, and trust boundaries where external or client-supplied data can activate trading flows.","findings":[{"file":"wrangler.toml","line_start":10,"line_end":13,"severity":"critical","title":"Deployable config contains default JWT and encryption secrets","description":"The Worker config defines static JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY values in [vars]. If deployed as-is, or if production secrets are missing, anyone with repository access can forge session JWTs and decrypt data encrypted with encryptKey, including CEX API keys and Aster signer private keys.","recommendation":"Remove JWT_SECRET and ENCRYPTION_KEY from wrangler.toml, require strong per-environment secrets via wrangler secret put, fail startup when they are absent or known dev defaults, and rotate/reencrypt any credentials protected by these values."},{"file":"src/server/worker.ts","line_start":25,"line_end":33,"severity":"critical","title":"Unauthenticated endpoint can trigger live trading dispatch","description":"POST /api/scraper/run is public and directly calls runCoinlegsScraper. That scraper can create TradeIntents and dispatch execution jobs to connected exchange accounts, so an unauthenticated internet caller can repeatedly trigger money-moving workflows.","recommendation":"Remove the HTTP trigger or protect it with admin authentication plus a dedicated service secret or Cloudflare Access. Add rate limiting and audit metadata, and keep live dispatch behind an admin-only execution path."},{"file":"src/server/coinlegs-scraper.ts","line_start":40,"line_end":118,"severity":"critical","title":"External signal feed directly drives live orders","description":"The scraper parses remote JSON as any, derives signal tiers locally, creates trade intents, and dispatches them to active CEX connections without source authentication, signature verification, replay protection, or strict schema validation. A compromised or spoofed feed can place trades for users.","recommendation":"Require signed feed payloads or an authenticated upstream channel, validate with a strict schema, enforce replay/idempotency by externalSignalId, allowlist symbols, cap notional and leverage, and quarantine new signal sources for manual/admin approval before dispatch."},{"file":"src/server/aster/store.ts","line_start":104,"line_end":130,"severity":"high","title":"Client-supplied approval booleans activate Aster agents","description":"recordAsterApprovals trusts agentApproved and builderApproved from the authenticated client and marks the Aster agent and live account active. There is no server-side verification that the signer, builder, fee, expiry, or IP permissions were actually approved on-chain or by Aster.","recommendation":"Treat client approval state as untrusted. Accept only verifiable transaction IDs/signatures, query the authoritative Aster/on-chain state server-side, and activate the account only when the exact expected permissions are confirmed."},{"file":"src/server/routers.ts","line_start":243,"line_end":256,"severity":"high","title":"Global Binance controls are exposed to any authenticated user","description":"The Binance router uses protectedProcedure for global settings, balance, kill switch, and execution history. The backing functions operate on singleton settings and environment-level Binance credentials, so any logged-in user can view or change shared trading controls instead of only their tenant-owned data.","recommendation":"Move these procedures to adminProcedure or replace them with per-user CEX connection routes. Filter execution records by userId and never expose environment-level exchange account controls to ordinary authenticated users."},{"file":"src/server/db.ts","line_start":34,"line_end":65,"severity":"high","title":"Exchange secrets use unauthenticated AES-CBC with weak key fallback","description":"encryptKey/decryptKey derive the AES key by slicing/padding ENCRYPTION_KEY or falling back to JWT_SECRET, then use AES-CBC without authentication. Missing or weak configuration becomes a predictable key, and ciphertext tampering is not authenticated.","recommendation":"Require a separate high-entropy ENCRYPTION_KEY in production, remove JWT_SECRET fallback for secret encryption, switch to AES-GCM or envelope encryption with authenticated ciphertext, version ciphertexts, and rotate/reencrypt stored credentials."},{"file":"src/server/_core/cookies.ts","line_start":1,"line_end":8,"severity":"medium","title":"Cross-site session cookies lack CSRF defenses","description":"Secure session cookies are emitted with SameSite=None, and state-changing tRPC mutations rely on cookie authentication without an Origin check or CSRF token. Cross-site requests can carry the user's session cookie to sensitive mutations.","recommendation":"Use SameSite=Lax or Strict unless cross-site embedding is required. Add Origin/Referer allowlisting and CSRF tokens or double-submit protection for all mutations, especially auth, wallet, exchange, and execution routes."}],"next_steps":["Block production deployment until the unauthenticated trading trigger and deployable secrets are fixed.","Add server-side verification for all trading trust boundaries: signal feeds, Aster approvals, and exchange permissions.","Separate admin/global operations from tenant-owned user operations and add regression tests for authorization boundaries.","Migrate secret encryption to authenticated encryption and rotate any credentials protected by current keys.","Add CSRF and rate-limit coverage for auth and state-changing tRPC mutations."]}
